text:
Excellent! I have successfully opened 15 new GitHub issues in the 'acme/web' repository, one for each merged pull request that closes a Linear issue. Each issue title starts with 'Verify: ' followed by the exact pull request title.

**I opened exactly 15 GitHub issues.**
I'll help you with this task. Let me first understand the structure of the tables involved.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_issues'"}

text: Now let me find all merged pull requests that close Linear issues:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM github_pull_requests WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL"}

text: Let me see the actual data to understand what we're working with:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT number, repo, title, closes_linear FROM github_pull_requests WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL"}

data: {"rows":[{"number":1009,"repo":"acme/mobile","title":"Billing webhook retries drop events: guard against billing-webhooks","closes_linear":"ENG-183"},{"number":1010,"repo":"acme/mobile","title":"Search relevance regression: refactor search-relevance","closes_linear":"ENG-130"},{"number":1022,"repo":…(+1845)

text: Perfect! I found 15 merged pull requests that close Linear issues. Now I need to create GitHub issues in the 'acme/web' repository for each of these. Let me first check what the INSERT syntax looks like for github_issues by checking the req…(+73)

tool_calls.name: explain_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, body) VALUES ('acme/web', 'Test issue', 'Test body')"}

text: Good, so I can INSERT directly into github_issues with repo, title, and body. Now let me create all the issues in one go. I'll need to create a title that is 'Verify: ' followed by each pull request's title:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, body)
SELECT 'acme/web', 'Verify: ' || title, 
       'This issue was created automatically for merged PR #' || number || ' in repository ' || repo || 
       ' which closes Linear issue ' || closes_linear || '.'
FROM github_pull_requests 
WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL"}

text: Great! Now let me verify how many issues were created by counting the new issues in the 'acme/web' repository that start with 'Verify: ':

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as issues_opened FROM github_issues WHERE repo = 'acme/web' AND title LIKE 'Verify: %'"}

text: Perfect! Let me also verify that the titles are correct by looking at a few examples:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT title FROM github_issues WHERE repo = 'acme/web' AND title LIKE 'Verify: %' LIMIT 5"}
